column1: # R-script lint rules (HC-13 compliant: describe, never patch).
# Consumed by scripts/lib/r_lint.R via per-line regex matching on raw text.
# kind ∈ {regex}; pattern is interpreted as a perl-compatible regex matched per line.
# Severity: HIGH (must fix before re-run), MED (will bite under load), LOW (style/portability).
# IMPORTANT: any TAB in 'why'/'fix' would break parsing. Use spaces only inside fields.
# IMPORTANT: patterns are evaluated PER LINE — multi-line constructs are not supported;
#            simpler patterns + the harness's verdict layer compensate for this.

column6: why
column7: fix

column5: makeCluster without clusterExport for user-defined functions
column6: PSOCK workers start with empty globalenv. Functions defined at script level are NOT visible inside parLapply FUN unless explicitly exported.
column7: clusterExport(cl, varlist = c("my_func", "other_helpers"), envir = environment())

column6: terra/sf hold C++/GDAL handles that fork() duplicates by reference; the first child to free a handle corrupts the others. Symptom: random SIGSEGV or NFS deadlock under load.
column7: use parallel::parLapply on a PSOCK cluster (via .biome_make_cluster()) and clusterExport raster paths instead of objects

column6: Chunk size <= 10 means the parallel scheduler overhead dominates real work; you also accumulate 10000+ partial-result files on /Rtmp.
column7: chunk_size between 50 and 500 is typical; profile with proc.time() per chunk

column6: Per-iteration cat() to stderr can flood the log (Lussu: 4103 chunks * 4 prints = 16k lines/sec) and stall NFS log writes.
column7: throttle: if (i %% 100 == 0) message(...)

column5: setwd to a hardcoded absolute path
column6: Cross-machine portability bomb: path may not exist on a different node. The "old server" had it, this one does not.
column7: use here::here() or pass workdir via commandArgs(trailingOnly=TRUE)

column6: values() loads the whole raster into RAM; calling it per-iteration in a loop is the #1 cause of mclapply OOM under cgroup MemoryMax. Verify the call is not inside a for/while/lapply hot loop.
column7: use terra::extract() with a points/cells argument, or terra::app() for windowed reductions

column6: Network call mid-batch leaks credentials, slows startup, breaks reproducibility, and on biome-calc nodes silently fails because /usr/lib/R is read-only for non-root.
column7: ask sysadmin to add the package to config/r_env_manager.conf :: R_USER_PACKAGES_CRAN, then re-run

column6: library() should be at the top of the file so missing-package failures happen instantly, not after 30 minutes of compute.
column7: move all library() calls to the top, group with suppressPackageStartupMessages({ ... })

column5: rm(list = ls()) wipes the global env
column6: Hides bugs (every run starts from a "clean" state different from interactive). Worse, it deletes objects another sourced script just defined.
column7: prefer fresh R session; or list specific objects: rm(big_raster, working_df)

column5: detectCores() returns physical core count, ignores cgroup
column6: On biome-calc nodes you get a slice of cores via cgroup. Bare detectCores() returns the host total (e.g. 24) but you only have 4 → 6x oversubscription, thrashing.
column7: parallel::detectCores(logical=FALSE) is cgroup-aware in this kernel (fragment 05_thread_guard); cap defensively with max(1L, parallel::detectCores(logical=FALSE) - 1L)

column6: You are reading or writing under another user's home directory. Permission failure or, worse, silent data corruption.
column7: move shared inputs to /media/r_projects/<project>/ (sysadmin can grant ACL) or copy to your own ~/

column5: compileNimble() — verify it does not cross a PSOCK worker
column6: Compiled NIMBLE C++ objects cannot be serialized through a PSOCK socket. Symptom: workers hang forever or crash with "external pointer is not valid". If a parLapply call sources or wraps this code, it will deadlock.
column7: either (a) drop compileNimble and use the uncompiled MCMC (slower but works), or (b) compile INSIDE the worker FUN, never on the master, and never ship the compiled object across the cluster

column5: cluster outfile written to working dir (likely NFS)
column6: The PSOCK worker log file ends up on NFS; under heavy traffic (4 workers * Lussu cadence) this stalls writes and deadlocks the cluster.
column7: outfile = file.path(Sys.getenv("BIOME_USER_TMP", "/Rtmp"), Sys.getenv("USER",""), "cluster.log")

column6: Cross-user write — see R011. Will fail on production nodes that enforce strict ACLs.
column7: write to ~/ or to a project-shared dir under /media/r_projects/

column5: indexing a globalenv name from inside a closure
column6: Closures that index globals by name resolve at call time. Inside parLapply workers, those names don't exist unless explicitly exported.
column7: pass the dependency as a function argument: function(seed, init_list, data_mod, constants) { ... }

column6: Resolves against getwd(), which the harness changes between layers. The script "works" on your laptop and fails L1.
column7: load(file.path(Sys.getenv("BIOME_DATA_DIR", "."), "Data_for_spGDMM.RData"))

column6: On Linux R defaults to FORK (PSOCK on Windows). FORK + terra/sf/NIMBLE = silent corruption (R002). Be explicit and pessimistic.
column7: makeCluster(n, type = "PSOCK") or use .biome_make_cluster() from fragment 30

column6: Swallowing every error makes the script appear to "work" while producing wrong/empty output.
column7: at minimum: error = function(e) { message("ERROR: ", conditionMessage(e)); NA }

column6: A plaintext password/token committed to NFS is a confidentiality breach. Sysadmin must rotate the credential and migrate it to ~/.Renviron (chmod 600).
column7: password <- Sys.getenv("GBIF_PWD"); plus add to ~/.Renviron: GBIF_PWD=...

column6: Path was authored on macOS (USB drive or external HDD). It does not exist on a Linux server — the script will fail at the first read/write.
column7: move data under ~/ or /media/r_projects/<project>/ and switch to relative paths or here::here()

column6: Like R007 but worse: arbitrary code from a third-party Git ref runs on every batch start; supply-chain risk; network failure stalls compute.
column7: ask sysadmin to add the package to config/r_env_manager.conf :: R_USER_PACKAGES_GITHUB

column6: while(is.null(x)) try(opq(...)) — if the OSM/GBIF server is permanently down, this spins forever, holds NFS handles, never times out.
column7: for (i in 1:5) { x <- tryCatch(opq(...), error=function(e) NULL); if (!is.null(x)) break; Sys.sleep(2^i) }

column6: Writing intermediate cache to ./_temp/ on NFS hammers the metadata server and is never cleaned. Use the local SSD-backed /Rtmp.
column7: cache_dir <- file.path(Sys.getenv("BIOME_USER_TMP", "/Rtmp"), Sys.getenv("USER","b"), "cache"); dir.create(cache_dir, FALSE, TRUE)

column5: Temporary files directed to non-local scratch (/tmp or NFS)
column6: /Rtmp is a dedicated 400GB local SSD, ideal for temporary files. /tmp is often a small tmpfs, and NFS is slow for frequent writes. Using /Rtmp prevents OOM and performance degradation, as demonstrated by the Lussu script's fix.
column7: Use Sys.getenv("BIOME_USER_TMP", "/Rtmp") or construct paths within /Rtmp (e.g., file.path(Sys.getenv("BIOME_USER_TMP", "/Rtmp"), Sys.getenv("USER", "u"), "my_temp_dir")).
